goal: Create an adaptable, rotating weekly set of desk-friendly stress-management techniques (Week A-D) to prevent habituation.
constraints: Techniques take under a few minutes and require no special equipment.
Chamomile allergy: avoid chamomile-based rituals or beverages.
Ergonomics probably helps long-term — better lumbar support, keyboard angle — but that's not an immediate trick.
decisions: Adopt six discreet, desk-friendly moves for discreet stress management (Square Breath, Under-desk Clench, Palm Press, Pocket Stone, Silent Name, Toe Ground).
Chewing gum briefly before turning camera on, then stash the gum
Weekly rotation plan: rotate through Week A-D blocks weekly to prevent habituation.
open: Pare down to a go-to 3-step routine
Format as a tiny desk card
Experiment with lunch journaling (three-sentence reflection) during lunch
Experiment with desk plant or succulent and observe mood impact
Experiment with discreet gum use during video calls
Window Pause (60-90s): Stand, look outside (sky/trees/buildings), take three slow diaphragmatic breaths.
3-Minute Loop (3-5min): Quick walk around the block or up/down a stairwell; if you can't leave, walk in place quietly at your desk.
River Visual (60-90s): Close your eyes or gaze at a nature photo and mentally 'walk' your river route while breathing slowly.
Experiment with chamomile-free tea rituals for calm
Adopt rotating weekly technique packs (Week A-D) and schedule switches.
